final_answer: To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, it is important to stay up to date on your COVID-19 vaccine to prevent serious illness [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Ensuring good indoor airflow can help prevent the spread of the virus [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Practicing physical distancing is also recommended to reduce the risk of transmission [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Wearing a mask in appropriate settings can protect yourself and others [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00001]. Regular handwashing with soap and water for at least 20 seconds is crucial [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Using an alcohol-based hand sanitizer with at least 60% alcohol when needed is advised [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Covering your coughs and sneezes with a tissue or your elbow is important [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Avoiding close contact with anyone who is sick or has symptoms is recommended [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023]. Staying home if you feel ill or have COVID-19 symptoms is essential [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00022]. Finally, testing for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure is advised [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].
